page: Vultr vx1-g-192c-768g is an x86_64 architecture server within the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features 192 shared vCPUs manufactured by AMD and 768.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 4.0 GB of RAM per virtual core. The server includes one complimentary public IPv4 address and does not feature any GPU accelerators.

A primary characteristic of this instance is its shared CPU allocation, meaning the 192 vCPUs share physical host resources with other tenants. Additionally, local storage is limited to 1 GB of network-attached storage. These factors represent a tradeoff, as workloads requiring sustained, predictable CPU performance or high-speed local disk I/O will need to account for potential resource contention and rely on external network storage volumes.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the shared CPU model allows Vultr to offer a massive 768.0 GB memory footprint at a lower hardware class tier compared to dedicated-core alternatives. This configuration is suitable for memory-intensive workloads with bursty or non-continuous CPU utilization, such as large-scale staging environments, in-memory caching, and distributed data processing tasks that prioritize memory capacity over dedicated compute cycles.
description: The Vultr vx1-g-192c-768g is an x86_64 server within the VX1 Cloud Compute family, featuring 192 shared AMD vCPUs and 768.0 GB of RAM. This configuration provides a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core, but relies on a shared CPU allocation model and offers only 1 GB of network-attached local storage with no GPU accelerators. The shared resource model provides a cost-effective option for memory-heavy deployments that do not require dedicated, sustained CPU performance. It is best suited for memory-intensive workloads with variable CPU demands, such as large-scale caching, staging environments, and distributed data processing tasks. The instance also includes one complimentary public IPv4 address, making it a viable option for non-critical, high-capacity memory deployments.
og_description: Vultr vx1-g-192c-768g is a VX1 Cloud Compute server with 192 shared AMD vCPUs, 768 GB RAM, and 1 GB network storage. It is designed for memory-intensive workloads with variable CPU demands.
meta_description: Vultr vx1-g-192c-768g is a VX1 Cloud Compute server featuring 192 shared AMD vCPUs, 768 GB of RAM, and 1 GB of network storage for memory-heavy, bursty workloads.
tagline: A high capacity shared core instance providing substantial memory and virtual cores for non continuous memory intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 192 shared AMD vCPUs on x86_64 architecture
768.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core
Shared CPU allocation model for cost-efficient resource scaling
Suitable for large-scale caching and memory-bound staging environments
